parsed_review.summary: CP00-397 is a clean, descriptor-only continuation of the RP13 payments-core series (CP00-392..396). It covers exactly the 10 planned RP13 units RP13.P03.M05.S05 -> RP13.P03.M05.S14 (micro M05 "Permission And Audit Binding"), risk class A, deviation_from_plan=false, with deliverable distribution security_audit:2, contract:2, implementation:2, test:3, hermes_evidence:1 that agrees across the live closeout-pack-plan (packs[0]), manifest plan_binding_snapshot, review-baseline, registry PAYMENTS_CORE_CP397_REQUIREMENTS, and manifest delivery_counts, and is enforced by validatePaymentsCoreCp397Coverage (validators.js:994-1027). The implementation (registry.js:86-101/720-765, service.js:682-801, validators.js:972-1139) produces only frozen descriptor objects via pure functions carrying exhaustive no-write/no-runtime/no-real-data flags; there are no state writes, no DB/object-storage access, no Hermes runtime receipts, no payments/matching/AR-aging/journal runtime, and no command/UI/API execution. Tests (model.test.js:386-434) and the contract validator (validate-rp13-payments-core-contract.mjs:332-362, deep-equality on contract projections current_pack, no_write_attestation, p03_permission_slice_descriptor, cp397_requirements, cp397_no_write_attestation) cover coverage, descriptor-only rows, authority boundaries, evidence packets, and the CP00-398 / RP13.P03.M05.S15 handoff. The production_ready claim is appropriately conservative: manifest and command-evidence both set production_ready=false with status implemented_pending_review and runtime_ready=false, exposing only production_ready_candidate=true at the Hermes packet level. The authority boundary holds: descriptor.authority_boundary.claude_is_final_approval=false, no_write_attestation.promotes_claude_to_final_approval=false, claims_enterprise_trust_from_local_validation=false, human_final_approval_required=true; the review request is read-only (Read/Grep/Glob) and Hermes patterns are used only for deterministic review packet/receipt descriptors (receipt_shape descriptor_only..._no_write), not product runtime. Review-evidence hardening checks pass: readiness auth preflight succeeded (exit 0, 1436-byte valid JSON result; no auth failure, no 0-byte/malformed/fenced/tool-call-shaped output), validation-report shows forbidden_tools_present=false and active_untracked_implementation_files_omitted empty, and no invalid review attempts are recorded. Post-review artifacts (claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, construction-inspection.json) are absent at review time by design, matching the closed CP00-396 structure and the command-evidence post-review-gated-command notes; this is expected, not missing evidence. The 7 modified tracked files match the active implementation refs (index.js needs no change due to export *), and unrelated dirty files (docx, workbook/*, ldip invalid-attempt json) are correctly excluded from scope. No P0/P1/P2 issues found; one cosmetic P3 on changed-file-scope dual-listing carried over from CP00-396. Recommend proceeding to post-review adjudication and human final approval (Claude does not grant final approval or enterprise-trust closure).

findings.title: changed-file-scope lists the pack's own output dir and handoff doc as both implementation and unrelated-preserved
findings.details: docs/closeout-packs/cp00-397/ (the pack's own manifest/command-evidence output directory) and docs/closeout-pack-plan/new-session-handoff.md each appear simultaneously in untracked_implementation_files (lines 131-132) and in unrelated_dirty_files_preserved (lines 137-138). This is a cosmetic inconsistency in the scope categorization: a file/directory cannot be both an in-scope implementation/output artifact and an 'unrelated' preserved file. It has no effect on review integrity because the decision-relevant field active_untracked_implementation_files_omitted is empty (nothing in-scope was hidden from review), and the substantive pack outputs (manifest.json, command-evidence.json) were inspected directly. The identical issue was reported and accepted as non-blocking P3 in CP00-396 (CP00-396-P3-01), so the generator behavior is unchanged.
findings.recommendation: In the changed-file-scope generator, treat the pack's own docs/closeout-packs/<pack>/ directory and the closeout-pack-plan handoff doc as in-scope implementation/output artifacts only, and exclude them from unrelated_dirty_files_preserved so the two lists are mutually exclusive. Non-blocking for CP00-397 closeout.
findings.post_review_disposition: Open, non-blocking. Cosmetic metadata-only labeling issue in the review scope descriptor; does not affect pack production-readiness, no-write/no-real-data boundaries, authority boundary, or closeout gating.
